description_long: This object is a vintage cathode-ray tube (CRT) television with a boxy, rectangular form and a faux woodgrain exterior. The front features a large, convex glass screen surrounded by a thick, black plastic bezel, with a control panel on the right side that includes two rotary dials, two toggle switches, and a speaker grille. The top and sides are finished in a wood-patterned laminate, giving it a classic retro appearance. The back panel is made of black plastic with multiple ventilation slots, a protruding section for the CRT, and several ports and switches for connectivity. The television sits on small, integrated feet and has a sturdy, utilitarian build typical of mid-to-late 20th-century home electronics. The overall design is utilitarian, with visible wear and patina that suggest long-term use in an urban or domestic setting.
description: A vintage CRT television with a woodgrain exterior, large convex glass screen, and a side control panel with dials and switches.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large convex screen, black bezel, control panel with dials and switches, and speaker grille.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the woodgrain finish and the depth of the television's body.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing the black plastic back panel with ventilation slots, CRT housing, and connectivity ports.
description_view_3: Right side view highlighting the continuous woodgrain finish and the television's boxy profile.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick black plastic bezel with rounded corners and a matte finish.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of rotary dials and toggle switches on the front right panel.
